title: Evidence Ledger
description: Structured record of claims used in marketing decisions, with type (fact / inference / hypothesis / recommendation), behavioral strength, sources, downstream uses, and a kill rule. Required for decision-grade outputs (pricing, channel commitment, hiring, scaling). Inline citations are sufficient for quick-mode answers.

subject.description: What this ledger is about — product/segment/decision context.

as_of.description: Date the ledger was last updated. Memories rot; refresh quarterly or before major decisions.

id.description: Stable short id used to reference this entry from copy or assets, e.g. 'wtp-pilot-3'.

claim.description: The claim itself, in one sentence. State the claim as a falsifiable proposition, not as marketing copy.

type.description: Per the SKILL.md output contract: facts are observed; inferences combine facts; hypotheses are unverified; recommendations propose action.

confidence.description: Subjective confidence 0..1. Use 0.9+ only for first-party behavioral evidence.

segment.description: ICP segment this claim applies to. A claim valid for one segment may be false for another.

origin.description: URL, interview ID, CRM record, or internal source name.

retrieved_at.description: Date the source was inspected. Sources older than 6 months should be re-verified before load-bearing decisions.

quote.description: Exact quote or numeric value. Paraphrase loses falsifiability.

used_in.description: Asset ids or section names that load-bear on this claim — e.g. 'hero', 'pricing-page-tier-2', 'outbound-template-v3'.

kill_rule.description: What evidence would invalidate this claim. Without a kill rule the claim cannot be wrong, which means it is not load-bearing.

supersedes.description: Entry ids this claim replaces. Track refutation chains rather than silently overwriting.
